A set $S$ of distinct positive integers has property \textbf{ND} if no element $x$ of
$S$ divides the sum of the integers in any subset of $S \setminus \{x\}$. Here
$S \setminus \{x\}$ means the set that remains after $x$ is removed from $S$.

\begin{enumerate}[(a)]

\item Find the smallest positive integer $n$ such that $\lbrace 3, 4, n
      \rbrace$ has property \textbf{ND}.
\item If $n$ is the number found in (i), prove that no set $S$ with property
      \textbf{ND} has $\lbrace 3, 4, n \rbrace$ as a proper subset.

\end{enumerate}
